title: Business Email Setup
shortDesc: Professional branded email on your domain, configured correctly and secured from the start.
description: A business email address on your own domain builds trust with clients and protects your brand. I handle the full setup — DNS records, mail provider configuration, SPF, DKIM, and DMARC — so your emails reach the inbox rather than the spam folder every time.
bannerAlt: Email client showing a professional branded inbox on a custom domain

body.text: A misconfigured email domain does not just look unprofessional — it gets your messages silently dropped into spam folders, or rejected outright. SPF, DKIM, and DMARC are the three DNS records that tell receiving servers your domain is legitimate. Getting them right is not optional.

items: Google Workspace (Gmail with your domain) — best for teams already in the Google ecosystem
Microsoft 365 (Outlook with your domain) — preferred for businesses using Office tools
Zoho Mail — a cost-effective option for smaller teams
Custom SMTP with Postfix or similar — for full self-hosted control

body.text: I configure your MX, SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records, verify delivery with a test send to multiple providers, and document every record I created so you can manage your domain confidently in the future. If you need email forwarding, aliases, or shared inboxes, those are included.

benefits.title: Inbox deliverability
benefits.description: Properly configured SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records mean your emails land in inboxes, not spam folders.

benefits.title: Professional brand image
benefits.description: A branded email address signals legitimacy to clients and partners in a way free webmail cannot.

benefits.title: Secure by default
benefits.description: Email security settings are configured to prevent spoofing and phishing attacks on your domain from day one.

benefits.title: Works with your existing tools
benefits.description: Compatible with Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, or any IMAP provider — your team keeps the apps they prefer.

whyMe: Email setup sounds simple until a misconfigured DNS record sends every message to spam. I have set up email infrastructure for dozens of businesses and know the pitfalls. I also test deliverability after setup, so you have confidence before sending your first important message.
